editableNewDialogue.text: Quick check: if I’m meal-prepping meats, what’s the simplest way to keep cooked portions from drying out in the fridge?

Two easy tactics: (1) store portions with a little cooking liquid/fat (pan juices, butter, or broth) in a tightly sealed container, and (2) reheat gently—low power in the microwave or covered in a pan with a splash of water/broth. Also, slicing after reheating (instead of before) helps retain moisture.
Does it matter if I cool it down before sealing the container?

Yes: let it cool briefly so steam doesn’t condense into water inside the container (which can affect texture), but don’t leave it out too long. A practical rule is to portion it, let it steam off for ~10–20 minutes, then seal and refrigerate.
Unrelated: why do some recipes say to salt steak an hour ahead, but others say do it right before cooking?

Salt timing changes what happens on the surface. Right before cooking mainly seasons the exterior. Salting ~45–60+ minutes ahead draws out moisture, then that salty liquid gets reabsorbed, seasoning a bit deeper and helping browning. The “worst” window is salting and cooking within ~5–40 minutes, when moisture is drawn out but not yet reabsorbed.
Random trivia time—give me a couple of odd food facts.

1) The world’s most expensive spice by weight is saffron; it takes a huge number of crocus flowers to produce a small amount. 2) Honey can remain edible for extremely long periods because it’s low in water and acidic; archaeologists have found ancient honey that was still preserved.
Could you make the week plan feel less repetitive—swap in lamb mince on a couple of the ground-beef days and keep the rest roughly the same so it still hits the minimum calories?

Yes—if you sub lamb mince for two of the ground-beef lunches (keeping the same gram amounts and adjusting with a little extra butter if needed), you’ll get more variety while staying at or above 2400 calories per day. Want the lamb days placed on Day 3 and Day 7, or different days?
Just to clarify: I’m not actually doing carnivore long-term. I was only using it as a template for that shopping list—I'm not exclusively eating animal products.
Got it—you’re not following a strict carnivore diet; you just wanted a carnivore-style example based on that list, and you’re not exclusively eating animal products.
